title: Chain rule with tables
topic_path: Differentiation: composite, implicit, and inverse functions

assessment_items.question_content:
The following table lists the values of functions $f$ and $g$, and of their derivatives, $f'$ and $g'$, for the $x$-values $-2$ and $4$.

$x$ | $f(x)$ | $g(x)$ | $f'(x)$ | $g'(x)$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$-2$ | $5$ | $-1$ | $1$ | $6$
$4$ | $-4$ | $-2$ | $0$ | $8$

Let function $F$ be defined as $F(x)=f\Bigl(g(x)\Bigr)$.

$F'(4)=$ [[Ōśā numeric-input 1]]
hints: In order to find $F'(4)$, we should first find an expression for $F'(x)$ (for any input $x$) in terms of $f$, $g$, and their derivatives.

So $F'(x)=f'\Bigl(g(x)\Bigr)\cdot g'(x)$.

We can now plug $x=4$ into this expression and evaluate. Here is the table of values again, for your convenience:

$x$ | $f(x)$ | $g(x)$ | $f'(x)$ | $g'(x)$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$-2$ | $5$ | $-1$ | $1$ | $6$
$4$ | $-4$ | $-2$ | $0$ | $8$

Let's plug in those values!

$\begin{align}
F'(4)&=f'\Bigl(g(4)\Bigr)\cdot g'(4)
\\\\
&=f'(-2)\cdot 8&&\gray{g(4)=-2\text{ , }g'(4)=8}
\\\\
&=1\cdot 8&&\gray{f'(-2)=1}
\\\\
&=8
\end{align}$
In conclusion, $F'(4)=8$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following table lists the values of functions $f$ and $g$, and of their derivatives, $f'$ and $g'$, for the $x$-values $-2$ and $1$.

$x$ | $f(x)$ | $g(x)$ | $f'(x)$ | $g'(x)$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$-2$ | $10$ | $1$ | $-7$ | $4$
$1$ | $-2$ | $-1$ | $4$ | $5$

Let function $G$ be defined as $G(x)=f\Bigl(g(x)\Bigr)$.

$G'(-2)=$ [[Ōśā numeric-input 1]]
hints: In order to find $G'(-2)$, we should first find an expression for $G'(x)$ (for any input $x$) in terms of $f$, $g$, and their derivatives.

So $G'(x)=f'\Bigl(g(x)\Bigr)\cdot g'(x)$.

We can now plug $x=-2$ into this expression and evaluate. Here is the table of values again, for your convenience:

$x$ | $f(x)$ | $g(x)$ | $f'(x)$ | $g'(x)$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$-2$ | $10$ | $1$ | $-7$ | $4$
$1$ | $-2$ | $-1$ | $4$ | $5$


Let's plug in those values!

$\begin{align}
G'(-2)&=f'\Bigl(g(-2)\Bigr)\cdot g'(-2)
\\\\
&=f'(1)\cdot 4&&\gray{g(-2)=1\text{ , }g'(-2)=4}
\\\\
&=4\cdot 4&&\gray{f'(1)=4}
\\\\
&=16
\end{align}$
In conclusion, $G'(-2)=16$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following table lists the values of functions $g$ and $h$, and of their derivatives, $g'$ and $h'$, for the $x$-values $1$ and $2$.

$x$ | $g(x)$ | $h(x)$ | $g'(x)$ | $h'(x)$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$1$ | $-3$ | $2$ | $-1$ | $3$
$2$ | $-2$ | $6$ | $3$ | $5$

Let function $H$ be defined as $H(x)=g\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)$.

$H'(1)=$ [[Ōśā numeric-input 1]]
hints: In order to find $H'(1)$, we should first find an expression for $H'(x)$ (for any input $x$) in terms of $g$, $h$, and their derivatives.

So $H'(x)=g'\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\cdot h'(x)$.

We can now plug $x=1$ into this expression and evaluate. Here is the table of values again, for your convenience:

$x$ | $g(x)$ | $h(x)$ | $g'(x)$ | $h'(x)$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$1$ | $-3$ | $2$ | $-1$ | $3$
$2$ | $-2$ | $6$ | $3$ | $5$
Let's plug in those values!

$\begin{align}
H'(1)&=g'\Bigl(h(1)\Bigr)\cdot h'(1)
\\\\
&=g'(2)\cdot 3&&\gray{h(1)=2\text{ , }h'(1)=3}
\\\\
&=3\cdot 3&&\gray{g'(2)=3}
\\\\
&=9
\end{align}$
In conclusion, $H'(1)=9$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following table lists the values of functions $g$ and $h$, and of their derivatives, $g'$ and $h'$, for the $x$-values $0$ and $-3$.

:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$-3$ | $14$ | $-12$ | $-7$ | $3$
$0$ | $2$ | $-3$ | $-1$ | $3$

Let function $F$ be defined as $F(x)=g\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)$.

$F'(0)=$ [[Ōśā numeric-input 1]]
hints: In order to find $F'(0)$, we should first find an expression for $F'(x)$ (for any input $x$) in terms of $g$, $h$, and their derivatives.

So $F'(x)=g'\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\cdot h'(x)$.

We can now plug $x=0$ into this expression and evaluate. Here is the table of values again, for your convenience:

$x$ | $g(x)$ | $h(x)$ | $g'(x)$ | $h'(x)$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$-3$ | $14$ | $-12$ | $-7$ | $3$
$0$ | $2$ | $-3$ | $-1$ | $3$
Let's plug in those values!

$\begin{align}
F'(0)&=g'\Bigl(h(0)\Bigr)\cdot h'(0)
\\\\
&=g'(-3)\cdot 3&&\gray{h(0)=-3\text{ , }h'(0)=3}
\\\\
&=-7\cdot 3&&\gray{g'(-3)=-7}
\\\\
&=-21
\end{align}$
In conclusion, $F'(0)=-21$.

$H'(4)=$ [[Ōśā numeric-input 1]]
hints: In order to find $H'(4)$, we should first find an expression for $H'(x)$ (for any input $x$) in terms of $f$, $h$, and their derivatives.

We can now plug $x=4$ into this expression and evaluate. Here is the table of values again, for your convenience:

$x$ | $f(x)$ | $h(x)$ | $f'(x)$ | $h'(x)$

H'(4)&=f'\Bigl(h(4)\Bigr)\cdot h'(4)

$F'(3)=$ [[Ōśā numeric-input 1]]
hints: In order to find $F'(3)$, we should first find an expression for $F'(x)$ (for any input $x$) in terms of $f$, $g$, and their derivatives.

So $F'(x)=f'\Bigl(g(x)\Bigr)\cdot g'(x)$.

We can now plug $x=3$ into this expression and evaluate. Here is the table of values again, for your convenience:

$x$ | $f(x)$ | $g(x)$ | $f'(x)$ | $g'(x)$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$0$ | $0$ | $-3$ | $2$ | $1$

$\begin{align}
F'(3)&=f'\Bigl(g(3)\Bigr)\cdot g'(3)

assessment_items.question_content:

:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$1$ | $4$ | $2$ | $3$ | $-2$
$2$ | $6$ | $1$ | $1$ | $0$

Let function $G$ be defined as $G(x)=f\Bigl(g(x)\Bigr)$.

$G'(2)=$ [[Ōśā numeric-input 1]]
hints: In order to find $G'(2)$, we should first find an expression for $G'(x)$ (for any input $x$) in terms of $f$, $g$, and their derivatives.

So $G'(x)=f'\Bigl(g(x)\Bigr)\cdot g'(x)$.

We can now plug $x=2$ into this expression and evaluate. Here is the table of values again, for your convenience:

$x$ | $f(x)$ | $g(x)$ | $f'(x)$ | $g'(x)$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$1$ | $4$ | $2$ | $3$ | $-2$
$2$ | $6$ | $1$ | $1$ | $0$


Let's plug in those values!

$\begin{align}
G'(2)&=f'\Bigl(g(2)\Bigr)\cdot g'(2)
\\\\
&=f'(1)\cdot 0&&\gray{g(2)=1\text{ , }g'(2)=0}
\\\\
&=3\cdot 0&&\gray{f'(1)=3}
\\\\
&=0
\end{align}$
In conclusion, $G'(2)=0$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following table lists the values of functions $g$ and $h$, and of their derivatives, $g'$ and $h'$, for the $x$-values $-1$ and $4$.

$x$ | $g(x)$ | $h(x)$ | $g'(x)$ | $h'(x)$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$-1$ | $12$ | $4$ | $-8$ | $-2$
$4$ | $-3$ | $-11$ | $2$ | $8$

**Evaluate $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[g\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\right]$ at $x=-1$.**

[[Ōśā numeric-input 1]]
hints: In order to evaluate $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[g\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\right]$ at $x=-1$, we should first find an expression for $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[g\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\right]$ (for any input $x$) in terms of $g$, $h$, and their derivatives.

So $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[g\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\right]=g'\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\cdot h'(x)$.

We can now plug $x=-1$ into this expression and evaluate. Here is the table of values again, for your convenience:

$x$ | $g(x)$ | $h(x)$ | $g'(x)$ | $h'(x)$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$-1$ | $12$ | $4$ | $-8$ | $-2$
$4$ | $-3$ | $-11$ | $2$ | $8$

Let's plug in those values!

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}g'\Bigl(h(-1)\Bigr)\cdot h'(-1)
\\\\
&=g'(4)\cdot (-2)&&\gray{h(-1)=4\text{ , }h'(-1)=-2}
\\\\
&=2\cdot (-2)&&\gray{g'(4)=2}
\\\\
&=-4
\end{align}$
In conclusion, the value of $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[g\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\right]$ at $x=-1$ is $-4$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following table lists the values of functions $g$ and $h$, and of their derivatives, $g'$ and $h'$, for the $x$-values $-2$ and $3$.

$x$ | $g(x)$ | $h(x)$ | $g'(x)$ | $h'(x)$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$-2$ | $0$ | $3$ | $1$ | $-5$
$3$ | $5$ | $3$ | $1$ | $5$

**Evaluate $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[g\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\right]$ at $x=-2$.**

[[Ōśā numeric-input 1]]
hints: In order to evaluate $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[g\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\right]$ at $x=-2$, we should first find an expression for $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[g\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\right]$ (for any input $x$) in terms of $g$, $h$, and their derivatives.

So $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[g\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\right]=g'\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\cdot h'(x)$.

We can now plug $x=-2$ into this expression and evaluate. Here is the table of values again, for your convenience:

$x$ | $g(x)$ | $h(x)$ | $g'(x)$ | $h'(x)$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$-2$ | $0$ | $3$ | $1$ | $-5$
$3$ | $5$ | $3$ | $1$ | $5$

Let's plug in those values!

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}g'\Bigl(h(-2)\Bigr)\cdot h'(-2)
\\\\
&=g'(3)\cdot (-5)&&\gray{h(-2)=3\text{ , }h'(-2)=-5}
\\\\
&=1\cdot (-5)&&\gray{g'(3)=1}
\\\\
&=-5
\end{align}$
In conclusion, the value of $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[g\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\right]$ at $x=-2$ is $-5$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following table lists the values of functions $f$ and $h$, and of their derivatives, $f'$ and $h'$, for the $x$-values $0$ and $2$.

$x$ | $f(x)$ | $h(x)$ | $f'(x)$ | $h'(x)$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$0$ | $3$ | $2$ | $-5$ | $-3$
$2$ | $1$ | $0$ | $3$ | $1$

**Evaluate $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[f\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\right]$ at $x=0$.**

[[Ōśā numeric-input 1]]
hints: In order to evaluate $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[f\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\right]$ at $x=0$, we should first find an expression for $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[f\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\right]$ (for any input $x$) in terms of $f$, $h$, and their derivatives.

So $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[f\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\right]=f'\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\cdot h'(x)$.

We can now plug $x=0$ into this expression and evaluate. Here is the table of values again, for your convenience:

$x$ | $f(x)$ | $h(x)$ | $f'(x)$ | $h'(x)$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$0$ | $3$ | $2$ | $-5$ | $-3$
$2$ | $1$ | $0$ | $3$ | $1$

Let's plug in those values!

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}f'\Bigl(h(0)\Bigr)\cdot h'(0)
\\\\
&=f'(2)\cdot (-3)&&\gray{h(0)=2\text{ , }h'(0)=-3}
\\\\
&=3\cdot (-3)&&\gray{f'(2)=3}
\\\\
&=-9
\end{align}$
In conclusion, the value of $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[f\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\right]$ at $x=0$ is $-9$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following table lists the values of functions $f$ and $h$, and of their derivatives, $f'$ and $h'$, for the $x$-values $-3$ and $-1$.

$x$ | $f(x)$ | $h(x)$ | $f'(x)$ | $h'(x)$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$-3$ | $6$ | $5$ | $7$ | $-8$
$-1$ | $8$ | $-1$ | $-3$ | $-4$

**Evaluate $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[f\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\right]$ at $x=-1$.**

[[Ōśā numeric-input 1]]
hints: In order to evaluate $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[f\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\right]$ at $x=-1$, we should first find an expression for $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[f\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\right]$ (for any input $x$) in terms of $f$, $h$, and their derivatives.

So $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[f\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\right]=f'\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\cdot h'(x)$.

We can now plug $x=-1$ into this expression and evaluate. Here is the table of values again, for your convenience:

$x$ | $f(x)$ | $h(x)$ | $f'(x)$ | $h'(x)$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$-3$ | $6$ | $5$ | $7$ | $-8$
$-1$ | $8$ | $-1$ | $-3$ | $-4$

Let's plug in those values!

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}f'\Bigl(h(-1)\Bigr)\cdot h'(-1)
\\\\
&=f'(-1)\cdot (-4)&&\gray{h(-1)=-1\text{ , }h'(-1)=-4}
\\\\
&=-3\cdot (-4)&&\gray{f'(-1)=-3}
\\\\
&=12
\end{align}$
In conclusion, the value of $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[f\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\right]$ at $x=-1$ is $12$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following table lists the values of functions $f$ and $g$, and of their derivatives, $f'$ and $g'$, for the $x$-values $0$ and $1$.

$x$ | $f(x)$ | $g(x)$ | $f'(x)$ | $g'(x)$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$0$ | $7$ | $3$ | $-3$ | $-4$
$1$ | $6$ | $0$ | $1$ | $-2$

**Evaluate $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[f\Bigl(g(x)\Bigr)\right]$ at $x=1$.**

[[Ōśā numeric-input 1]]
hints: In order to evaluate $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[f\Bigl(g(x)\Bigr)\right]$ at $x=1$, we should first find an expression for $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[f\Bigl(g(x)\Bigr)\right]$ (for any input $x$) in terms of $f$, $g$, and their derivatives.

So $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[f\Bigl(g(x)\Bigr)\right]=f'\Bigl(g(x)\Bigr)\cdot g'(x)$.

We can now plug $x=1$ into this expression and evaluate. Here is the table of values again, for your convenience:

$x$ | $f(x)$ | $g(x)$ | $f'(x)$ | $g'(x)$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$0$ | $7$ | $3$ | $-3$ | $-4$
$1$ | $6$ | $0$ | $1$ | $-2$


Let's plug in those values!

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}f'\Bigl(g(1)\Bigr)\cdot g'(1)
\\\\
&=f'(0)\cdot (-2)&&\gray{g(1)=0\text{ , }g'(1)=-2}
\\\\
&=-3\cdot (-2)&&\gray{f'(0)=-3}
\\\\
&=6
\end{align}$
In conclusion, the value of $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[f\Bigl(g(x)\Bigr)\right]$ at $x=1$ is $6$.

Let's plug in those values!

The following table lists the values of functions $g$ and $h$, and of their derivatives, $g'$ and $h'$, for the $x$-values $-3$ and $2$.

$2$ | $4$ | $-3$ | $3$ | $-4$

**Evaluate $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[g\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\right]$ at $x=2$.**

[[Ōśā numeric-input 1]]
hints: In order to evaluate $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[g\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\right]$ at $x=2$, we should first find an expression for $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[g\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\right]$ (for any input $x$) in terms of $g$, $h$, and their derivatives.

So $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[g\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\right]=g'\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\cdot h'(x)$.

We can now plug $x=2$ into this expression and evaluate. Here is the table of values again, for your convenience:

$x$ | $g(x)$ | $h(x)$ | $g'(x)$ | $h'(x)$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$-3$ | $14$ | $-8$ | $-7$ | $6$
$2$ | $4$ | $-3$ | $3$ | $-4$

Let's plug in those values!

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}g'\Bigl(h(2)\Bigr)\cdot h'(2)
\\\\
&=g'(-3)\cdot (-4)&&\gray{h(2)=-3\text{ , }h'(3)=-4}
\\\\
&=-7\cdot (-4)&&\gray{g'(-3)=-7}
\\\\
&=28
\end{align}$
In conclusion, the value of $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[g\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\right]$ at $x=2$ is $28$.

[[Ōśā numeric-input 1]]
hints: In order to evaluate $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[g\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\right]$ at $x=0$, we should first find an expression for $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[g\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\right]$ (for any input $x$) in terms of $g$, $h$, and their derivatives.

So $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[g\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\right]=g'\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\cdot h'(x)$.

We can now plug $x=0$ into this expression and evaluate. Here is the table of values again, for your convenience:

$x$ | $g(x)$ | $h(x)$ | $g'(x)$ | $h'(x)$
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$0$ | $5$ | $2$ | $-1$ | $1$
$2$ | $11$ | $8$ | $7$ | $5$

Let's plug in those values!

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}g'\Bigl(h(0)\Bigr)\cdot h'(0)
\\\\
&=g'(2)\cdot 1&&\gray{h(0)=2\text{ , }h'(0)=1}
\\\\
&=7\cdot1&&\gray{g'(1)=7}
\\\\
&=7
\end{align}$
In conclusion, the value of $\dfrac{d}{dx}\left[g\Bigl(h(x)\Bigr)\right]$ at $x=0$ is $7$.
